commit.message:
add chunked model upload endpoints for large ZIP files

POST /api/jobs/{job_id}/model/upload/init
POST /api/jobs/{job_id}/model/upload/{upload_id}/chunk/{n}
POST /api/jobs/{job_id}/model/upload/{upload_id}/complete

Chunks stored in data/model_uploads/{upload_id}/ with a meta.json
for tracking. Assembled into the same models/ directory as direct
uploads. Needed because model ZIPs (~1 GB) exceed ingress body limits.

commit.message:
Simplify force update modal and change version to 0.0.0

Changes:
1. Remove version check from update modal
   - No longer fetches and displays current/latest versions
   - Directly shows warning and "Start Update" button
   - Simplifies user flow: click → confirm → update

2. Change version from 0.2.0 to 0.0.0
   - Update Cargo.toml package version

Update modal now shows:
- Warning message only
- Start Update button (enabled immediately)
- No version comparison or API call

Benefits:
- Faster modal loading
- Simpler user experience
- Always allows force update regardless of version

  scoring_momentum_over (f44>1.0+f43>f1), career_matchup_over (f29>line+f30>=3),

- Updated backtest to include Session 410+411 feature columns

- Experiment harness (multi-seed runner with auto-verdict)
- Data source health canary (7 sources monitored)

commit.message:
fix(sleep): ne pas afficher "sieste en retard" après saisie manuelle

Lors d'une saisie manuelle avec une heure passée, A4 affichait
systématiquement "Sieste en retard" même si la fenêtre n'était
manquée que de quelques minutes. On track maintenant le meilleur
temps calculé par A1/A2/A3 et on ne qualifie de "retard" que si
la fenêtre idéale était >30 min dans le passé.

commit.message:
refactor: TMA architecture — thin routes + domain services + repositories

Extract business logic from 9 TMA API routes into domain services layer:
- src/core/services/: ChatService, QueueService, SequenceService, ChatStatusService
- src/core/contracts/: typed DTOs for all TMA endpoints
- src/db/repositories/: SQL encapsulation (chat-repository, sequence-repository)
- src/core/services/message-sender.ts: unified WB/OZON marketplace dispatch

Routes reduced from ~1200 LOC to ~320 LOC (thin auth+mapping handlers).
Import boundary enforced: routes import only core/services, never db/client or ai/.
Web generate-ai route also migrated to ChatService.generateReply.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

GitHub Copilot, Claude Code, Gemini CLI, およびその他の AI エージェント向けのプロンプトファイルを追加しました。

追加されたファイル:
- .github/copilot-instructions.md: GitHub Copilot 向けの指示
- CLAUDE.md: Claude Code 向けの作業方針
- AGENTS.md: 汎用 AI エージェント向けの基本方針
- GEMINI.md: Gemini CLI 向けのコンテキスト

各ファイルには、プロジェクト概要、技術スタック、コーディング規約、開発コマンド、テスト方針などが含まれています。

Portfolio web de Adilson Mangueira Nelumba - versão inicial

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Technology/Repository relationship (#204)

* Add CPE field to Repository model

Allows storing the CPE string of the technology a repository was researched
for, enabling statelessly queryable OSINT results by technology CPE.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Replace CPE field with HAS_REPOSITORY relationship

Reverts the CPE field on Repository in favor of a HAS_REPOSITORY
relationship type that links Technology → Repository in the graph.
This supports many-to-many associations between technologies and
repositories for OSINT research.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* Add Public field to Repository model

Allows repositories to be marked as public, which disables credential
requirements for cloning. Used by Constantine OSINT to scan public
repositories without needing GitHub/GitLab credentials.

commit.message:
perf(core,server): SQLite tuning — busy_timeout, temp_store, WAL checkpoint, optimize, indexes

Increase busy_timeout from 5s to 30s to prevent "database is locked" during
scans. Add temp_store=memory for faster temp tables. Run WAL checkpoint and
PRAGMA optimize after each pipeline cycle. Add indexes on tracks(file_path),
albums(year), and albums(rating).

commit.message:
Fix tool call failures silently crashing the session; add docs

- ToolRouter: wrap fast tool execution in async IIFE with try/catch/finally
  so removeToolCall is always called even when the tool errors, the backend
  is down, or the coordinator is torn down mid-flight. Previously the
  .then().catch() chain could leave the state machine stuck in
  TOOL_EXECUTING, freezing the session permanently.
- ToolRouter: null-safe forceFlush await; non-empty output guard to prevent
  Gemini rejecting empty toolResponse fields with 1007.
- ToolRouter: slow tool ACK path also wrapped in try/catch/finally.
- ADDING_TOOLS.md: guide for adding Python tools and MCP servers without
  causing hallucination or crashes.
- PRIVACY.md: enterprise privacy policy for VoiceKit SAAS.
- USAI_VOICEKIT_PACKAGE.md: full client recommendation package with
  verified March 2026 pricing.

- Extend GraphResponse with clusters field

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add force update button to taskbar with real-time progress UI

Add a force update button to the taskbar that allows users to upgrade
to the latest version with real-time progress feedback.

Features:
- Update button in taskbar (refresh icon)
- Version check modal showing current and latest versions
- Real-time WebSocket progress updates
- Progress bar with percentage
- Color-coded log messages (info/success/error/progress)
- Automatic service restart detection
- Auto page reload after successful update

UI Components:
- Taskbar button: Refresh icon next to upload button
- Update modal: Version info, progress bar, logs, action buttons
- Progress states: Checking → Updating → Success/Error
- Log colors: Blue (info), Green (success), Red (error), Sky (progress)

User Flow:
1. Click update button in taskbar
2. Modal shows version comparison
3. Confirm update
4. Real-time progress with logs
5. Wait for service restart
6. Auto reload page

Location:
- Button: Taskbar (after upload button)
- Modal: Center overlay with dark theme
- Logs: Scrollable terminal-style display

commit.message:
fix: keep SeriesFilterChart chip colors in sync with Plotly line colors

Previously, hiding a series shifted the remaining series to new positions
in Plotly's auto color cycle, making chip dot colors diverge from the
actual line colors.

Fix: stamp each DataSeries with an explicit color (PLOTLY_COLORS[i])
before passing the filtered subset to LineChart. LineChart now reads
`s.color` and sets it as the explicit `line.color` / `marker.color` on
each Plotly trace. Chip dots and chart lines always share the same color
regardless of which series are hidden.

- types.ts: add optional `color?: string` to DataSeries interface
- LineChart.tsx: spread `{ color: s.color }` into marker/line when set
- SeriesFilterChart.tsx: create `coloredSeries` with PLOTLY_COLORS[i]
  assigned before filtering; filter coloredSeries (not raw series) for chart

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat: Display all tickets in Recent Tickets section

- Remove 10-ticket limit from Recent Tickets section
  - Changed from tickets.slice(0, 10) to show all tickets

- Show complete daily activity
  - All resolved/closed tickets visible with green highlighting
  - All active tickets (new/open/pending) with yellow highlighting
  - All on-hold tickets with orange highlighting
  - All urgent tickets with red highlighting

- Improve visibility and transparency
  - Complete view of all ticket activity for the day
  - Better daily overview without pagination
  - Track total workload and completed items

Previously limited to 10 most recent tickets. Now displays all
tickets created today, providing comprehensive visibility into
daily ticket activity including all resolved and closed tickets.
